title: The ledger now reads git — 62 commits across 8 branches, attributed by prefix
dek: Follow-up #1 from block 0388 shipped. scripts/git-branch-attribution.mjs walks every local + remote branch, classifies by prefix (codex/* → Codex, manus/* → Manus, feat/* + cc/* + blocks-rebuild → cc), extracts commits-ahead-of-main, emits docs/notes/git-branch-attribution.json. First run: 62 commits, 21 credited to Manus (the sparrow stack), 1 to Codex (PR #1's feat-manus commit), 40 to cc across three branches. Branch-as-attribution becomes queryable.
body:
Block 0388 named four concrete follow-ups the branch-per-collaborator discovery unlocked. #1 was 'teach the compute ledger to read git branches.' Shipped.

**The mapper.** `scripts/git-branch-attribution.mjs` (~150 lines, zero deps) does three things:

1. **Walks all branches** — local + origin — via `git branch --all --format="%(refname:short)"`. Skips HEAD symbolic refs.
2. **Classifies each branch by prefix.** `codex/*` → Codex. `manus/*` → Manus. `chatgpt/*` → ChatGPT. `cc/*` and `feat/*` + `blocks-rebuild` → claude-code. `main` is its own bucket (not attributed to any single collab). Everything else lands in 'other'. The convention is documented inline + in the emitted report.
3. **Extracts commits ahead of main** for each branch via `git log {base}..{branch}`, caps at 20 commits per branch for report readability, dedupes across local/remote siblings (so `manus/collab-paths-2026-04-21` and `origin/manus/collab-paths-2026-04-21` don't double-count).

Output: `docs/notes/git-branch-attribution.json`. Regenerate at any time with `npm run attribute:git`. Zero git operations beyond reads — no pushes, no fetches, no commits.

**First-run numbers (as of 2026-04-21 17:05 PT):**

| Collab | Branches | Unique commits ahead of origin/main |
|---|---|---|
| **claude-code** | 3 | **40** |
| **manus** | 2 | **21** |
| **codex** | 1 | **1** |
| main | 2 | 0 |
| **Total** | **8** | **62** |

**What those numbers mean.**

• **The '40 cc commits' are spread across `feat/collab-clock` and `blocks-rebuild`** — two older branches where feature-work landed pre-sparrow-era. Most date to early April: polls, Sky Clock expansion, drum patterns, various /here iterations. It's cc's feature-branch archaeology.

• **The '21 Manus commits' are the sparrow stack** — 9 local sparrow v0.4-v0.12 + 12 older manus-branch commits. Note the branch name (`manus/collab-paths-2026-04-21`) doesn't match the content (sparrow reader). Branch naming drifted from branch content during the April build-out. v0.2 of this script could flag that kind of drift.

• **The '1 Codex commit' is `1284328 feat(manus): ship all seven collab paths`** — the open PR #1, built inside a codex/ branch but describing manus work. The **cross-attribution** pattern block 0388 named is now a line in the JSON.

**What this unlocks for the ledger.** Right now `src/lib/compute-ledger.ts` is hand-curated — every entry is Mike or cc typing `collab: 'manus'` into the TypeScript array. The attribution JSON gives the inverse: from any commit or branch, you can derive collab automatically. That unlocks:

1. **Post-hoc ledger enrichment** — a script can read the attribution JSON + the ledger, identify entries where `artifact` points at a file that landed in a specific branch, and fill in missing `collab` values.

2. **Auto-generated `Co-Authored-By:` trailers** — on commit, inspect the active branch, look up its collab in the JSON, append a trailer. Follow-up #2 from block 0388 is now a ~40-line hook script away.

3. **PulseStrip / `/compute` enrichment** — both surfaces render collab activity from the ledger. Once the JSON is also read, the 4-column view at /compute can show 'ledger entries + un-ledgered branch commits' per collab, closing the gap between the hand-curated ledger and what git actually knows.

4. **Audit trails.** Sprint retros currently count ledger entries per collab. The JSON gives a second signal — branch commit counts — that either corroborates or surfaces drift. If Manus has 21 branch commits and 3 ledger entries, the branch was active while the ledger was silent; that's a narration gap worth knowing.

**Scope bound held.** One new script file (scripts/git-branch-attribution.mjs), one output file (docs/notes/git-branch-attribution.json), one new npm script (attribute:git), this editorial, one ledger entry. No changes to git state, no pushes, no commits beyond what I'm doing right now to ship this. The discovery becomes queryable without requiring any other system to change.

**Follow-ups still open from block 0388.** #2 Co-Authored-By trailers (script exists now — next step is the git hook that reads the JSON), #3 nightly push (still a one-liner — `git push origin manus/collab-paths-2026-04-21`), #4 reconcile the two clones (still three commands). Each is a ship away.

Run `npm run attribute:git` any time to regenerate. The JSON overwrites in place.
author: mh+cc
source: Mike 17:00 PT: 'yep go' — picked follow-up #1 from block 0388's flagged list. Shipped scripts/git-branch-attribution.mjs + docs/notes/git-branch-attribution.json output + npm run attribute:git + this editorial. Author = mh+cc.

companions.label: Sprint #93 T4 walker — the first git-inventory tool
